Patch Set 1:

There's a question of how this should be handled. If someone does something like:

lnetctl net add --net tcp --if eth1
lnetctl net add --net o2ib --if inf1
lnetctl udsp add --src tcp --priority 0
my_gds_application --start

Should this fail explicitly? Or silently fallback to o2ib?

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> There's a question of how this should be handled. If someone does something like:
> 
> lnetctl net add --net tcp --if eth1
> lnetctl net add --net o2ib --if inf1
> lnetctl udsp add --src tcp --priority 0
> my_gds_application --start
> 
> Should this fail explicitly? Or silently fallback to o2ib?

LNet will already "silently fallback" to other interfaces for the purposes of selecting "healthier" interfaces, so, in my view, it is perfectly acceptable for there to be other exceptions to the selection priority. Ideally it is just documented clearly somewhere.

commit.subject: LU-19013 lnet: only send GDS traffic on capable interfaces
commit.message:
LU-19013 lnet: only send GDS traffic on capable interfaces

Only send GDS p2pdma traffic on interfaces that support
it. LNDs that implement lnd_get_dev_prio() support GDS,
hence filter NIs in lnet_get_best_ni() based on whether
this callback is implemented.

If GDS traffic is unintendedly sent over an incompatible
interface, the LND could associate the message with
garbage data and corrupt the file being written to.

This can most easily be replicated by using UDSP
to send gdsio traffic over an incompatible interface.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-lnet
Signed-off-by: Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>
Change-Id: Ic3b4f915b8345ea6570c105d11182659b62c129d
